title: Love Yourself Like Your Life Depends on It

chapters.title: Loving yourself unlocks a good life
chapters.text:
Famed neuroscientist Richard Bandler, who co-created Neuro-Linguistic Programming, was once able to ease severe schizophrenia symptoms in a matter of hours. Word got around, and soon doctors and clinic specialists were calling him for help. In one memorable case, a doctor reached out about a patient plagued by snake hallucinations. The hospital staff had tried every tool in their kit, yet nothing helped. The man had reached the point where he had to be physically tied to his bed, because snakes filled his vision no matter where he looked.

Keep a chosen light switch nearby for the dark moments, in Kamal Ravikant's words.

Bandler drove into town and rented a barrel of rubber snakes, along with three trained live snakes, two cobras and a python. He slipped into the shower area where patients normally bathed and tossed the rubber snakes all around. Then he placed the live snakes close to the man's wheelchair. Bandler rolled the patient into the shower and stepped away. The man yelled at the top of his lungs, snakes, snakes, snakes.

When Bandler stepped back in, he asked the patient to say which snakes were real and which were not. To everyone's surprise, the man pointed at the rubber ones and said rubber snakes, gestured around the room and called those hallucinated snakes, and pointed at the python and called it real snake.

Bandler was shocked to learn that the patient could spot hallucination from reality. He could even tell fake from real. As he rolled him out, Bandler asked how the patient had picked out the hallucinated ones. The patient explained that the hallucinated snakes looked see-through. He had known the truth the whole time, but his fear of snakes was so intense that he had slipped away from reality. Bandler then coached him back to focusing on what was real, and the hold of the hallucinations loosened.

When fear comes calling, do not wrestle with it. Look for your light instead.

Some days life feels easy and you imagine it will always be that way. Other days the wheels come off and it feels like you are stuck in a loop with no exit. Finding your switch can quiet or even tame your fears and phobias. The switch can appear in a flash or creep up over time, but it is there, waiting for you to discover it.

chapters.title: Only by loving yourself will life love you back
chapters.text:
You are probably wondering why this is about loving yourself rather than simply liking or accepting yourself. Love reaches our most basic instincts, before reason or good judgment. It goes back to babyhood, something natural and built into every person. Evolution has wired the human mind to recognize, accept, and return love. The body also understands how welcoming and nourishing love can be. Both mind and body respond to it on instinct. That instinct is why the word love has such a strong hold on us, shaping our feelings and steering our actions.

Make self-love your only truth. Then it will not be a thought you can take in or push away. It will be the only truth you live by. It is fine if you cannot find any sign of self-love right now. Once you accept that you need to love yourself more, you can start the journey toward self-appreciation. It will lead you to find real value in your existence. Practice celebrating every win so you can remind the neurons in your brain of the feeling.

The human mind recognizes and craves love on a fundamental level.

As you practice self-love, you will notice that life gets kinder to you as you appreciate yourself more. Watch how things start working out the way they should, and celebrate each time it happens. Soon, you will experience surprising moments that become a big part of your life.

Picture yourself in a dark room with a cloudy window, but a bright world outside. What do you do? Try cleaning the window so the light can come in. Darkness is the absence of light, so let the light in and let the darkness fade.

Now picture that darkness as your negative thoughts. How do you get rid of the feeling? Do you let it overwhelm you, or do you try to control it? Do you let it shape your every action, or do you hold it back and refuse to get stuck?

The window is the thing standing between you and the light. To keep dark thoughts from taking over, clean the window and let the light in. Find that window, since it is what is keeping you from the light, and fix it. Kamal Ravikant found his window through three methods. The first is the mental loop. Spot the negative thoughts that keep popping up in your head. If you do not want them to take over your life, try replacing them with positive ones and looping those instead. The second is meditation. Put on headphones, play soothing music, and meditate for seven minutes a day. Give yourself space to reflect and let your subconscious mind take care of itself. The third is one question. When you are with others and find yourself in a tricky situation, ask yourself this. If I loved myself deeply, would I allow this into my life? Would I let myself experience this?

chapters.title: Mental loops are a real lever for self-love
chapters.text:
Kamal Ravikant once heard a speaker break down how the mental loop works. He took away the lesson that humans often miss the negative patterns that quietly shape their thoughts. We confuse the act of thinking with replaying memories of things that have already happened. The mind circles the same moments, dragging up feelings, and we feel we have no choice but to feel them again.

Self-love is like a groove carved into stone by flowing water. With enough time, density turns into a river. That is how the mind runs. Thinking about a single thing once does not give it any power over you. But if you keep spinning the same thought over and over, it eventually digs a groove in your mind. That repeated pattern eventually floods you, creating a river you cannot steer. This is the reason practicing focused mental loops counts as one of the keys to self-love.

The upside of discovering self-love is that you become more productive when you make a conscious effort to feel better about yourself.

Keep repeating I love myself to lay down a positive mental loop. Most of the old emotions that trouble you get swapped for new ones that slowly settle into your subconscious. The payoff takes time, so be patient. Nothing good comes overnight. It may take weeks or months, but you will notice a shift in your life. That shift will have you loving yourself, enjoying life, and feeling good, and life will start loving you back.

Love is an emotion, love is a feeling, love is a way of being, in Kamal Ravikant's words.

Self-love adds a fresh spring to your step, especially when you give yourself time. It pours renewed energy into you, and that energy sharpens your choices. In that state, you are free to slip up and learn, because you give yourself the room to be patient.

chapters.title: A steady meditation habit grows your self-awareness
chapters.text:
You do not need much to get going with meditation. Sliding it into your day is easier than it looks. Begin by picking music that calms you. The length of your sit depends on how long the music runs. You do not need any special gear to begin.

Meditating with a calming piece, ideally instrumental, can soften negative emotions, anxiety, and stress.

Run through these steps to meditate well. Slip on a pair of headphones and play music that stirs warm feelings. The choice of music is yours, so go with whatever works. Do not fence yourself into a single genre. Rest against a wall or window. Cross one leg over the other or sit up straight. Comfort is what counts. Close your eyes, smile, and picture a gorgeous scene in your mind. Let the image stretch as far as it can. Take slow, deep breaths and whisper to yourself that you love who you are becoming. Release the breath at even intervals. That release helps you empty out whatever is weighing on your mind. Repeat those last two steps in rhythm. When the music fades, gently open your eyes and notice the refreshed feeling that has settled over you.

The light stirs a sense of healing and positive energy in both your conscious and subconscious mind. Inhale and exhale as naturally as you can, riding the rhythm of the music. This focused practice demands your full attention. Meditation is peaceful and soothing, and over time it brings a sense of love and growth. With steady practice, the magic shows up.

It is simpler to stay home, repeat the words I love myself, and practice self-love than to deal with crowds of people outside. When negative feelings and a heavy mindset creep in, keep telling yourself that you are loved and special. You need to keep testing your self-love to make sure your daily choices stay healthy.

The National Association for Self-Esteem links some negative behaviors to a lack of self-love, including early sexual activity, alcohol and drug abuse, self-harm, and eating disorders.

chapters.title: Rewiring the brain toward self-love
chapters.text:
Our dark thoughts and emotions color our state on the spot. When you find yourself knee-deep in anger, pain, or fear, remind yourself that if you truly loved yourself, you would not let that feeling settle in. Return to loving yourself by replaying the I love myself song in your head. Your mind will respond, and you will slide back to where you want to be. It works like a charm, and you do not have to fight the dark feelings. The same principle covers old memories too.

The way you replay a memory has a lot to do with how you frame your thoughts out loud.

Ask any neuroscientist, and they will tell you memory is not carved in stone. The more you recall an event, the better you feel about what happened around it. The memory grows stronger in your mind with each pass.

Say you once had a relationship that ended suddenly a few years down the road. You were probably gutted. Your memory starts reshaping how you experience it today. If the relationship made you miserable, you will tend to remember only the rough patches. Those moments will eventually dominate your thoughts and feelings. By contrast, if you recall the relationship from a happy place, your mind will zoom in on the warm highlights.

It is the same memory, but your inner state shifts how it shows up in your awareness. Even more, it changes how that memory touches you in the present. Instead of letting the painful story run you, pick love. Walk yourself back to the love you carry for yourself. Do it on repeat. Love and rewire your mind. You will feel good as the changes land, rather than getting nervous. You will start to accept that life can be magical, and that wonderful things you never imagined can come to pass.

A fresh start is within reach. First, reconnect with what is real. When dark thoughts show up, pause and call them out. Most of what we fear is not real or important. When fear arrives, remind yourself it is not real and move on. That move can become a useful trigger for new habits. Figure out what works for you and stick with it.

chapters.title: Stay grounded in reality
chapters.text:
When things get tough, do not get lazy. Channel that same energy into staying healthy when you are not sick. Keep improving and keep loving yourself at the same time.

To keep your energy up, never drop your meditation practice. Focusing your mind helps you stay in control of your life.

Whenever you meditate, be open to questions that come up. There may be more of them as time goes on. Are we our thoughts, or just observers of them? It is fine not to have an answer to all of them. The mystery itself is fascinating, so it is okay if we do not know what role we play in our own thinking. What matters is that we carefully choose the direction of our thinking. Always stay in control of your thoughts. Pick what you want to think about and ward off everything else.

Choose the thought that transforms you and makes your life feel amazing. It could be I love you, as Kamal Ravikant chose, but feel free to make it your own. It is worth it.

It is a common misconception that you have to love others before you can love yourself. It is actually the opposite. How can you give something you do not have?

That is why you hear the same instructions every time before a plane takes off. In an emergency, put on your own oxygen mask first, then help others.

Being in a place of self-love makes you more aware of yourself, your relationships, your surroundings, and life as a whole.

Loving yourself helps you drop old patterns, thoughts, and ideas that limit you from reaching your fullest potential. You will feel a huge change entering your life. In time, your mind will choose to love you on its own. When that happens, you will see the world through the eyes of someone in love with their own being. You feel the cool breeze on your skin. You love yourself, you are happy, you are beautiful, and you cannot stop thinking about how much you love your life. You have accepted how wonderful you are and welcome the love the world is throwing right back at you. That feeling is magical.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Self-love is not just a feeling. It is an action. It is a choice. Self-love shapes how you see yourself and how you treat others. It is a way of relating to your life that involves understanding your mistakes and losses and talking to yourself about life without harsh judgment. It also helps us set healthy boundaries and make healthy choices. People who love themselves rarely suffer from anxiety or depression. Before you can receive love and respect from others, train your self-love. Just do not build self-love out of selfishness or a lack of care for others. Without self-love, you may be unable to succeed at what you do.

You have to remember one simple lesson. Love yourself. For some of us who may not know how, this summary guides you on how to get there. Let Kamal Ravikant's truth teach you what you need to learn. Practice fiercely and religiously. The one thing that makes all of this work is whether you vow to love yourself. Write your commitment on paper. Write down whatever speaks to you, and do not be too careful about your words. Just let it flow and make sure it comes from your heart and is in your own words. When the magic finally happens, it will blow you away. Self-love is your secret to a healthy and fulfilling life. Decide to love yourself every day and be deliberate about it. No matter what is going on around you, decide never to stop loving yourself.

Trust your instincts and believe in your decision-making. Create your own magic and let the world respond. Listen to a seven-minute piece of music you like every day, and think about how much you love yourself as you listen to it.
